primary_variables.matched_definition: Permanent loss of function through full gene knockout (for example CRISPR KO or deletion).
evidence: map cysteine reactivity changes caused by FH mutation (Fig. 2a).
proteomes were isolated from an immortalized HLRCC cell line (UOK262 FH−/−), and a rescue line in which reintroduction of FH gene reduces S-succination (UOK262WT, FH+/+; Fig. 2b).

primary_variables.matched_definition: Perturbation where an exogenous chemical compound or pharmacologic agent is administered.
evidence: For identification of fumarate-sensitive cysteines (Dataset S4), 2 mg of HEK-293 proteomes (1 mL, 2 mg/mL) were incubated with 1 mM fumaric acid (10 μL, 100 mM stock in DMSO) or vehicle (DMSO, 10 μL) overnight at room temperature
2 mg of HEK-293 proteomes (1 mL, 2 mg/mL) were incubated with 1 mM fumaric acid (10 μL, 100 mM stock in DMSO) or vehicle (DMSO, 10 μL) overnight at room temperature
An identical experiment performed using 1 mM succinic acid (10 μL, 100 mM stock in DMSO) was used to identify and control for potential non-covalent effects of fumarate

replicate_hints: three independent replicate measurements of cysteine reactivity in HLRCC cells
multiplex_channel_hints: conjugated to light (low fumarate proteomes: vehicle-treated HEK-293 or UOK262WT) or heavy (high fumarate proteomes: fumarate-treated HEK-293 or UOK262) diazobenzene biotin-azide (azo) tag
relative Intensity ratio (R) of light/heavy (L/H) isotopic pairs in the MS1 spectra

evidence: map cysteine reactivity changes caused by FH mutation
proteomes were isolated from an immortalized HLRCC cell line (UOK262 FH−/−), and a rescue line in which reintroduction of FH gene reduces S-succination (UOK262WT, FH+/+)
2 mg of HEK-293 proteomes (1 mL, 2 mg/mL) were incubated with 1 mM fumaric acid (10 μL, 100 mM stock in DMSO) or vehicle (DMSO, 10 μL) overnight at room temperature

base_candidates_ranked.reason: The study uses isoTOP-ABPP with light and heavy diazobenzene biotin-azide tags, pooled pairwise, and quantified via L/H ratios in MS1 spectra.
A separate whole-proteome abundance analysis was performed using spectral counting (label-free) to normalize the reactivity data.

modifier_candidates_ranked.reason: The primary goal is mapping cysteine reactivity changes using IA-alkyne probes and streptavidin enrichment.
